name: Geometry
domain: Euclidean and non-Euclidean theorems, curvature, symmetry groups, topology
core_insight: Space has intrinsic properties measurable without embedding. Curvature determines whether parallel lines meet. The Euler characteristic is a topological invariant that survives any smooth deformation — the deepest fingerprint of a shape.

key_equations.name: Gaussian Curvature
key_equations.why: Product of principal curvatures: intrinsic — survives bending without stretching

key_equations.name: Gauss-Bonnet Theorem
key_equations.why: Total curvature of a surface equals 2π times its Euler characteristic — topology controls geometry

key_equations.name: Pythagorean Theorem
key_equations.why: Foundational: right triangles, distance, and the metric structure of flat space

seed_prompt: ☆ What is the Euler characteristic? Topology answers before geometry. Curvature lives in the surface itself.

fourth_equation.name: Parallel Postulate
fourth_equation.formula: Through a point not on a line, exactly one parallel exists
fourth_equation.why: The one Euclidean axiom that can be replaced — doing so generates hyperbolic geometry (infinite parallels) or elliptic geometry (none), proving flat space is not logically necessary

explore_paths.question: What happens to geometry at quantum and cosmological scales?
explore_paths.why: General relativity shows geometry is dynamical — mass curves spacetime; F18 is F09 made physical and dynamic

explore_paths.question: Can geometric invariants describe the shape of data or mind?
explore_paths.why: Topology (F20) extends F09 beyond metric distances to qualitative shape — the Euler characteristic survives all deformation

explore_paths.question: Are symmetry groups the deepest language of geometry?
explore_paths.why: Klein's Erlangen program: geometry is the study of properties invariant under a symmetry group — geometry and symmetry are the same thing

explore_paths.question: What governs the proportions that appear in all geometric structures?
explore_paths.why: φ, π, and e all emerge from geometric constructions — proportion is geometry's numerical fingerprint

open_questions.question: Is spacetime fundamentally discrete or continuous at the Planck scale (~10^-35 m)?
open_questions.tension: General relativity treats spacetime as a smooth continuum. Quantum mechanics implies discreteness. Loop quantum gravity predicts discrete spacetime quanta (spin foam). String theory requires extra continuous dimensions. Causal set theory proposes discrete spacetime events as the fundamental ontology. No experiment can currently reach Planck scale.
open_questions.curiosity_hook: The question of whether the universe is ultimately a continuum or a lattice is the deepest geometric question in physics. The answer would unify quantum mechanics and general relativity — the two theories we know are both correct but incompatible.

open_questions.question: What is the global topology of the universe? Is it finite or infinite?
open_questions.tension: The observable universe is flat to within measurement precision (Ω ≈ 1). A flat universe could be infinite (Euclidean 3-space) or finite (3-torus, Klein bottle, etc.). CMB anomalies suggestive of finite topology have been found but not confirmed. The Poincaré dodecahedral space was proposed to fit CMB patterns.
open_questions.curiosity_hook: We live inside a geometric object whose global shape we cannot directly observe. The topology of the universe is the largest open geometric question there is.

open_questions.question: Can non-Euclidean geometry be the right framework for describing consciousness or concept space?
open_questions.tension: Tononi's IIT uses geometric concepts (qualia space); Friston's free energy principle uses information geometry (Fisher metric on probability manifolds). Gardenfors' conceptual spaces model cognition using hyperbolic geometry. These are provocative analogies — whether they are literal descriptions of mental geometry or just useful metaphors is unresolved.
open_questions.curiosity_hook: If concepts have geometric relationships — distance, curvature, topology — then minds have shapes. The geometry of thought would be a scientific question, not just a metaphor.

cross_domain_bridges.manifestation: Sacred geometry in architecture encodes geometric proportions believed to have symbolic or perceptual power
cross_domain_bridges.example: The Parthenon's proportions approximate the golden ratio; geodesic domes minimize surface area for enclosed volume

cross_domain_bridges.manifestation: Neural network weight spaces and latent spaces have geometric structure that determines generalization
cross_domain_bridges.example: The 'manifold hypothesis' in deep learning: real data lies on low-dimensional curved manifolds in high-dimensional space

cross_domain_bridges.manifestation: Crystal structures are the physical instantiation of mathematical symmetry groups — all 230 space groups are realized in nature
cross_domain_bridges.example: The 5-fold symmetry of quasicrystals was considered geometrically impossible until their discovery (Nobel 2011)

cross_domain_bridges.manifestation: The Tonnetz (Euler) maps harmonic relations geometrically; toroidal topology of the chromatic scale encodes music theory
cross_domain_bridges.example: Voice-leading in counterpoint minimizes geometric distance in pitch space — harmony as geometry

noise_signature.pattern: Degenerate metric — distances become zero between distinct points
noise_signature.diagnosis: The coordinate system has broken down; a singularity or coordinate singularity has been reached

noise_signature.pattern: Topological obstruction — a continuous map cannot exist between two spaces
noise_signature.diagnosis: The global topology of the spaces is incompatible; no smooth transformation can connect them without tearing

noise_signature.pattern: Curvature singularity — curvature diverges at a point
noise_signature.diagnosis: The geometric description has broken down; physical theory predicts something that the geometric framework cannot represent
